arts.ttl: Man Utd transfer news: Saudi Pro League to reject Marcus Rashford
arts.sum: The Saudi Pro League will not look to sign Marcus Rashford, who is looking for a way out of Manchester United . "I've seen how other players have left in the past and I don't want to be that person.

arts.ttl: Manchester United XI vs Spurs – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Manchester United make the trip to Tottenham in the Carabao Cup quarter-finals this evening. Ruben Amorim will want his team to build on the momentum of a dramatic derby win at Manchester City at the weekend. Trailing in the 88th minute, two late goals saw the Red Devils snatch the points at the Etihad. arts.ttl: Tottenham injuries: Every player out of Carabao Cup quarter-final against Man Utd
arts.sum: While Romero picked up a different injury (groin) to the foot issue he'd recovered from, Van de Ven re-aggravated his hamstring problem. Micky van de Ven and Cristian Romero returned sooner than expected for Tottenham's Premier League clash with Chelsea two Sundays ago, but neither player finished the contest.

arts.ttl: Man Utd injuries: Every player out of Carabao Cup quarter-final against Tottenham
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim has provided the latest Manchester United team news ahead of their Carabao Cup quarter-final against Tottenham. United head into Thursday's cup tie off the back of a dramatic Manchester Derby win, and the Red Devils are certainly faring better than their opponents on the injury front.

arts.ttl: Tottenham a ‘plausible destination’ for player – Big competition but Spurs could provide ‘relaunch’ opportunity
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are enduring an inconsistent season in the Premier League, as they find themselves at tenth in the table. Both Micky van de Ven and Cristian Romero have faced injury issues across the campaign and that has exposed Tottenham’s lack of depth and quality at centre-back, with Ben Davies having played in the role. arts.ttl: What happens now with Man Utd and Rashford?
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford has been the poster boy for Manchester United since he burst into the team, but is at greater risk than ever of leaving his boyhood club, writes Simon Stone.
